If the spirit has been bound into its lodging place (or into a fetish), then the Exorcist must make a Wits + Subterfuge roll (difficulty 8) and gain more successes than the binder did when tying the spirit to its location.

The Theurge need no longer focus to communicate with spirits upon mastering this Gift; she is now constantly aware of all that spirits do around her and can interact with them at will. While most spirit-business is hardly of note, she will notice any dramatic changes in the environment. This Gift is taught by any spirit.

System: If the Garou’s Gnosis equals or exceeds the Gauntlet, she can automatically see into the Umbra. Otherwise, she must roll Perception + Occult (difficulty of the Gauntlet) to pierce the invisible wall. Only one success is required. The effect lasts for an entire scene or until the character enters an area with a higher Gauntlet.

The Theurge may take things to and from the Umbra without having to dedicate them to herself (see Rite of Talisman Dedication, pg. 173). This includes humans and animals, both willing and unwilling. Garou often use this power to take loyal Kinfolk with them on Umbral quests or to heal injured kin — both body and soul — in mystic Glens. This Gift is taught by an Incarna avatar.

System: The werewolf must grasp the object or person he wishes to take to the spirit world. The player must then spend a number of Willpower points: one for small items, two for larger items (swords, etc.) and three for huge items (including people). He makes the usual Gnosis roll to pierce the Gauntlet and step sideways; if successful, both Theurge and the desired object or person pass into the Umbra. An unwilling target may resist with a Willpower roll; each success subtracts one from the Garou’s successes. A Theurge attempting to abduct an unwilling victim must achieve at least three successes on his Gnosis roll.

Any person taken to the Umbra, unless she has the ability to step sideways, must rely on the Garou to escape, or else find an area with a very thin Gauntlet, such as a caern. The Gauntlet rating must be 3 or lower for a victim to exit of his own accord. If he does exit, he may not reenter the Umbra, no matter what the Gauntlet rating. Some people can manage to be spit out of the Umbra by passing Wyldlings, but this is as often as not fatal.

The Theurge may drain power from a spirit to feed his own resolve. This Gift is taught by a spirit servant of the Uktena totem.

System: If the Garou succeeds in a resisted Gnosis vs. Gnosis roll while combating a spirit, the spirit loses one Power point per success for the rest of the scene. For every two points drained, the Garou gains a temporary Willpower point, but loses any points exceeding her maximum at the end of the scene.

The Garou can change a spirit’s form or purpose. This Gift is taught by a Chimerling.

System: The Garou must best the spirit in a resisted Gnosis vs. Gnosis roll. The Theurge’s difficulty is based on his goal (see below), while the spirit’s difficulty is the werewolf’s Gnosis.

Change — Difficulty
Characteristics (Willpower, Rage, Gnosis; one point changed per success) — 6
Disposition (Friendly, Neutral, Hostile) — 8
Type (Naturae, Elemental, Bane, etc.) — 10

With but a thought, the Theurge can shred an opponent’s human reasoning, giving him the effective mind of a beast. This Gift is taught by a spirit servant of the Griffin totem.

System: The Garou spends Gnosis (see below) and rolls Wits + Empathy (difficulty of the target’s Willpower + 3; maximum 10). If successful, the Garou can permanently destroy a target’s Intelligence Attribute. Two Gnosis points must be spent for each point of Intelligence destroyed, and the werewolf cannot destroy more points than she rolls successes. Additionally, the more Intelligence the target loses, the more animalistic his behavior becomes.

The Garou is able to resist the pain of his wounds and continue acting normally. This Gift is taught by a Bear-spirit.

System: The player spends one Willpower point; the character is not affected by wound penalties for the remainder of the scene.

The Philodox can smell what a subject really is; she can supernaturally pick up the scent of her target’s true nature. This Gift is taught by any spirit servant of Gaia.

System: The Garou can automatically tell when someone is a werewolf, and can detect vampires and faeries on a Perception + Primal-Urge roll (difficulty 8). Even mages can be detected (difficulty 9).

Philodox, as judges of the Litany, can sense if others speak truth or lies. This Gift is taught by a Falcon Gaffling.

System: The player rolls Intelligence + Empathy (difficulty is the subject’s Manipulation + Subterfuge). This Gift reveals only whether the subject speaks truth or falsehood; it doesn’t reveal the actual truth, whatever that may be.

The Garou can summon low-powered spirits of a certain type — animal, air, water — and may give them one, and only one command. Once the spirits have fulfilled this request, they depart. This Gift is taught by an Incarna avatar.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point and rolls Charisma + Leadership (difficulty is the spirits’ highest Willpower) for the character to summon low-powered spirits of the specified type. The Storyteller decides what spirits respond and what they are capable of doing.

Animals within sight, scent and earshot of the Garou follow his orders willingly and unconditionally (assuming he does not order their suicide). They understand his orders and want to carry them out. This Gift is taught by a Lion- or Falcon-spirit.

System: The player rolls Charisma + Animal Ken (difficulty depends on the scarcity of animals in the character’s vicinity). Even if animals are around, they might not be suitable for the task at hand; that doesn’t mean they don’t try to help, though, which can prove problematic in itself.

The Garou can command a target to speak the truth, despite the victim’s desires.

System: The Storyteller rolls Manipulation + Intimidation (difficulty is the victim’s Intelligence + Subterfuge) on the player’s behalf. On a successful roll, the werewolf forces a particular person to speak the truth about a specified subject. The subject has to spend two Willpower points to resist the command. If the Philodox’s roll fails, the subject refuses the command. If the roll botches, the subject may lie to the character (thus the need for the Storyteller to make the roll for the player).

A Philodox needs to keep a tight grip on his bestial side for fear of losing control and, perhaps worse still, allowing his passions to color his judgment. However, a Garou cannot deny his animal nature; rationality cannot reign alone. The Garou can calm or enrage himself in times of need. This Gift is taught by a Water-spirit.

System: The character concentrates and meditates on her role as arbitrator and fair judge among the Garou. The player makes a Gnosis roll (difficulty 8). For every success, one point of Willpower or Rage is gained, as needed. This self-control can only be used once per scene.

This Gift also allows a Philodox to avoid entering frenzy by putting the character in a profound state of calm. The player spends one Willpower point and the character won’t frenzy for the duration of an entire scene, no matter what happens, unless the character seeks to make the change. Alternately, if a frenzy is ever necessary, the player can spend one point of Rage and the character automatically becomes a killing machine.

By tapping into the racial subconsciousness of the Garou, the Philodox dredges up ancient facts and lore. This Gift is taught by an Ancestor-spirit.

System: The Garou must meditate for a short time, concentrating on the past. The player rolls Gnosis (difficulty 8, 1 for each point of Past Life that the Garou possesses).

At times a Philodox needs to rally Garou to achieve a goal, but as events in the Savage West prove, Garou can rarely agree on anything, let alone work together. This Gift helps a Philodox inspire other Garou to follow his cause, despite tribal or personal differences. This power is most appropriate for inspiring Garou, but also affects humans. This Gift is taught by a Falcon-spirit.

System: The character makes a speech before his audience. The player then rolls Manipulation + Leadership (difficulty is the highest Willpower of any dissenting targets).

Individuals who willingly follow the Philodox automatically rally to him and gain the benefits conferred by the Gift. If the player’s roll succeeds, Garou who disagree with the character’s plan feel compelled to aid him despite their differences of opinion (although the expenditure of a Willpower point may allow an individual to break from the pack). If the roll botches, even a Garou’s dedicated followers may abandon him.

Rallied Garou each receive an extra point of Willpower to be used in achieving the Philodox’s goal. They may also be quicker to frenzy if that suits the mission ahead (a call to battle, for example); only three successes on a Rage roll are required to frenzy. Rival Garou have better relations as well, and put their differences aside for the common goal; difficulties of Social rolls are reduced by two.

The effects of this Gift last until the Philodox’s mission is complete or for the remainder of the scene, whichever comes first. The Gift can be used to inspire packs, members of a sept or attendants at a moot, even native and European tribes can be convinced to work together, assuming that the Philodox’s goal is not another party.

The Philodox’s presence demands respect and submission. Should she succeed in a test of wills, she dominates her foe, causing humans to fall to their knees and wolves to roll onto their backs. This Gift is taught by a Wolf-spirit.

System: The player must score at least three successes more than the opponent in a resisted Willpower contest.

The Philodox compels an individual or pack of Garou to perform a mission that may not correspond to an individual’s desires. Only a handful of subjects can be affected by this Gift at one time. The Philodox’s order is so compelling that few can resist, assuming that it doesn’t defy Garou basic instincts. The leader could make a pack of European Garou restore a caern to its native owners, but could not make werewolves eat human flesh. This Gift is taught by an Incarna avatar.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Manipulation + Leadership (difficulty of the target’s Willpower or the highest rating in a group). Three Willpower points must be expended to resist the order, and doing so results in the loss of three Honor Renown. The command is compelling until the act is accomplished to the best of the Garou’s ability.

The Philodox’s word is so compelling that the earth, the very foundation upon which her people live, responds to her command. While in contact with earth or rock, the werewolf can summon a wall to move with her, defending her from all angles. This Gift is taught by an earth elemental.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point. The wall’s dimensions are three yards high, two yards long and one yard thick; it can soak damage as if it has 10 dice, and 10 Health Levels must be inflicted to penetrate it in any one spot. The wall lasts for one scene or until released back into the earth by the Garou.

The werewolf may speak with his animal brethren, whether docile, domesticated sheep, furious catamounts or cold-blooded rattlers. This doesn’t change the critter’s basic reactions — an enraged buffalo isn’t likely to spend time in conversation, and will probably attack regardless. This Gift is taught by a Nature-spirit.

System: The Garou rolls Charisma + Animal Ken (difficulty 6). This roll must be made for each type of animal and for each encounter.

The Galliard can summon aid by howling; werewolves even beyond the range of hearing sense the call and may answer. This Gift can enhance the effect of any of the normal Garou howls (see Howls, pg. 50); it can also be sounded to summon specific Garou for a moot. Finally, Call of the Wyld is often used at the beginning of revels and other events, to invigorate the participants. This Gift is taught by a Wolf-spirit.

System: The Garou rolls Stamina + Empathy (difficulty 6). The number of successes determines how far away the call can be heard and how stirring it is to those who hear it. The actual effects are determined by the Storyteller, but they should be linked to the type of howl made and the intent of the Garou. Some examples are: every two successes gives those at a revel extra dice to their Pools; Wyrm agents are distracted by the call and their difficulties are temporarily raised; all Garou in the area do not hesitate to respond to the Garou’s Call to Succor.

The Galliard can call a waking dream into existence, sharing it with selected characters and thereby allowing them to silently communicate. This Gift is taught by a Chimerling.

System: The Garou spends one Willpower point per sentient being chosen; if a target is unwilling, the player rolls Manipulation + Expression (difficulty of the victim’s Willpower). All those included in the dream may interact normally through the Mindspeak, though they can inflict no damage through it. Their real bodies can still act, though all Dice Pools are reduced by two. The Mindspeak ends when all the participants want it to, or on the turn the Galliard fails the roll against an unwilling member. The beings affected must be within line of sight.

This frightening cry acts as a siren song to Wyrm-creatures, drawing them to the howler. Galliards use this Gift to create ambushes or to flush prey from hiding. This Gift is taught by any spirit servant of Gaia.

System: The Garou makes a resisted roll of Manipulation + Performance vs. the Wyrm-creature’s Willpower (both rolls are difficulty 7). If the Wyrm-creature loses, it must come to the caller.

The Garou can make annoying yips, yelps and howls to divert the attention of his target. This Gift is taught by a Coyote-spirit.

System: The player rolls Wits + Performance (difficulty of the victim’s Willpower). Each success subtracts one from the target’s Dice Pool next turn.

The Galliard enters another’s dream and can thereby affect the course of that dream. The werewolf need not be close to the target; all she needs is to know or have seen the dreamer. This Gift is taught by a Chimerling.

System: The player rolls Wits + Empathy (difficulty 8). If the dreamer awakens while the werewolf is still within the dream, the Garou is thrown out of the dream world and loses a Gnosis point.

The Galliard can draw anyone to his side with a mere glance. This Gift is taught by a Snake-spirit.

System: The player rolls Appearance + Enigmas (difficulty of the target’s Willpower). The Garou needs three successes to bring the target to his side; fewer at least start the victim moving in the right direction.

The werewolf’s cry lets out the animal in the target’s heart, whipping him into a killing mood. This Gift forces shapechangers to take the war-form, sends vampires into frenzy and drives humans absolutely loco. This Gift is taught by a Wolverine- or Wolf-spirit.

System: The player rolls Manipulation + Leadership (difficulty of the target’s Willpower). Each success drives the victim into a violent rage for one turn.

The Galliard can create minor Moon Bridges through which he alone can travel. This allows him to cover short distances in one hundredth of normal travel time, even letting him vanish from a foe’s sight to immediately reappear behind the luckless victim. These are never the safest of routes (particularly as Lunes don’t guard these temporary bridges); the denizens of the Storm Umbra sometimes follow the Galliard’s trail and spill out into the physical world. This Gift is taught by a Lune.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point to create the bridge. The Moon Bridge lasts for only one passage, unless the player spends a permanent Gnosis point during its creation, in which case it lasts until the next full moon. The maximum distance that can be traversed by the bridge is the Garou’s Gnosis in miles.

The Galliard can spin a tale and draw others into it, whether they’re interested or not. This Gift is usually used at moots when telling old stories, allowing the participants to reenact the tales themselves. However, this Gift can also be used on the unwilling, forcing them to participate in a story of the Garou’s choosing. This Gift is taught by an Ancestor-spirit.

System: When using this Gift on the unwilling, the player must spend a Gnosis point and score three successes on a Manipulation + Performance roll (difficulty of the target’s Willpower). If successful, the player may tell a story and force the victim to perform exactly as the story’s character does. This effect lasts one turn per Gnosis point spent.

The Galliard with this Gift becomes the ultimate manipulator, capable of playing a person’s heartstrings like a fiddle just by talking to him. He could cause someone to fall in love with him (which won’t last, and the victim’s love may turn into justified resentment) or drive another to hate a target of his choosing (again, this won’t last, but might develop into a full-scale feud). This Gift is taught by a Coyote-spirit.

System: The player rolls Manipulation + Empathy (difficulty of the target’s Willpower). Success allows the werewolf to steer the emotions of any one individual. The more successes, the more quickly the emotions manifest and the more strongly they are felt. The Storyteller should give the Galliard extra dice for roleplaying this Gift especially well.

The Galliard can tap the very fabric of the Storm Umbra with her song, drawing forth a chaotic flurry of Wyld-energies to work a change on her environment or those caught within it. This Gift is taught by a powerful Wyld-spirit.

System: The player spends two Gnosis points and rolls Wits + Expression (difficulty of the local Gauntlet). If she scores at least one success, the Wyld’s force breaks through and washes over the general area she selects. The Galliard may specify her general desire for the change, but she can hardly control the randomness she calls forth. The extent of the change and the precision of the target depend on the roll; with only one success, the canyon walls surrounding the Black Spiral Dancers might sprout a coat of slippery blue moss, while five successes might change all the Dancers’ weapons into silver-fanged sidewinders. The Storyteller is encouraged to be creative with whatever change results, but should remember that this Gift’s power usually manifests in beneficial ways — for the Moon Dancers, that is.

The Ahroun is usually the leader of the pack in combat, and this Gift strengthens that role. By channeling his courage and resolve, the Ahroun increases the resolve of his brethren. This Gift is taught by a Wolf-spirit.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point for his comrades to automatically receive one success on any Willpower rolls made during the scene. Note that this Gift does not affect its possessor.

The werewolf may sharpen his claws into virtual straight-razors. This Gift is taught by a Cat- or Wolf-spirit.

System: The player spends a Rage point while the werewolf takes a turn to scratch his talons on a stone or similarly hard surface. His successive claw attacks then do an additional die of damage for the remainder of the scene.

With this Gift, the Garou need not know the meaning of the word “impossible.” Shooting an arrow through a two-foot spot one hundred paces, ricocheting a bullet around a corner or even firing down the barrel of an opponent’s gun — the Ahroun can do them all. However, this Gift can only indirectly do any harm to a living thing — by shooting out a rope holding a chandelier, for instance. More direct usage is considered unsporting by the spirits, who will not permit such vulgarity. This Gift is taught by an Air-spirit.

System: The player adds her character’s permanent rating in Glory to her Dice Pool for the trick shot. The shot must be declared prior to rolling, and only for a truly bizarre attempt (simply saying “I shoot ‘im in the head!” doesn’t count).

The cagey fighter knows when his opponents are packing something truly dangerous, and the Ahroun have learned to scent out the most dangerous weapons of all. This Gift is taught by a Lune.

System: The Garou rolls Perception + Primal-Urge (difficulty 7). If successful, she can detect the general presence of silver; three successes allow her to pinpoint the silver’s location.

The werewolf can attack first in any given turn, no matter what his initiative roll may be. This Gift is taught by a Cat-spirit.

System: There is no mandatory point cost or roll for this Gift, but the player may spend Gnosis to ensure extra speed. If more than one Garou combatant has this Gift, the one who spends the most Gnosis goes first. If both (or all) spend the same amount, they take their first actions simultaneously.

The Ahroun can overwhelm a particular foe with the magnitude of his power, scaring the fool into inaction. This Gift is taught by a Fear-spirit.

System: The player rolls Strength + Intimidation (difficulty of the target’s Willpower). Each success cows the enemy for one turn; the victim cannot attack and may try to retreat. He may defend himself and otherwise act normally (though he will be scared).

The Ahroun can lock away his anger in order to maintain control, allowing him to fight and utilize Rage without as much chance of going into frenzy. However, once the floodgates are opened, his anger may well overpower him as it returns. This Gift is taught by a Bear- or Wolf-spirit.

System: The player rolls Willpower, difficulty of his own permanent Rage. Every two successes add one to the werewolf’s frenzy difficulties for the scene. Once the scene has passed, the Ahroun’s anger returns in force; he must spend one Willpower point or immediately make a frenzy check at regular difficulty.

Few Ahroun care to rely on silver bullets to put down renegade Garou; a more deadly option is to turn one’s own claws into silver and let instinct take over. This Gift is taught by a Lune.

System: The player rolls Gnosis (difficulty 7). Success transmutes the werewolf’s claws into silver, allowing her to inflict aggravated damage which can’t be soaked by Garou. However, silver hurts. Each turn her claws remain silver, she gains an automatic Rage point; furthermore, all noncombat difficulties are increased by one because of the distraction. When her Rage points exceed her Willpower, she must check for frenzy.

The Ahroun’s jaws can become a virtual bear trap, almost impossible to pry loose without the Ahroun’s consent. This Gift is taught by a Wolf-spirit.

System: The Ahroun must successfully bite his foe; after this, the player spends a Rage point to make his grip unbreakable. For each successive turn he opts to maintain the grip, he makes a bite attack roll (difficulty 3). While foes can make a resisted Strength roll to break the grip, the Garou may add half her Willpower to her Dice Pool.

The werewolf with this Gift has learned to use his Rage as efficiently as possible, and can draw an incredible amount of power from its reserves. This Gift is taught by a Wolverine-spirit.

System: The Garou regains one Rage point every turn in which he takes a Health Level of damage, and need not check for frenzy. In addition, the Garou can spend one Rage point per turn without losing that point. However, if more than one Rage point is spent that turn, the point is lost normally.

An Ahroun with this Gift can inspire his comrades so fiercely, they’d follow her through Perdition itself if need arose. This Gift is taught by a Wolf-spirit or an Incarna avatar.

System: The player spends a point of Willpower and rolls Charisma + Leadership (difficulty 8). Each success gives each of the Ahroun’s allies within 100 feet a bonus point of Willpower; this extra Willpower lasts for the rest of the scene. These points are used like any other, but can raise the ally’s Willpower above its maximum renewable 10. This Gift may be used only once per scene.

The werewolf calls on his ties to the sun, becoming immune to all natural forms of fire (although artificial fires like gas fires can still harm him). Additionally, the werewolf can ignite small parts of his body and keep them burning for extended periods. Ahroun most commonly use this Gift to light their manes during rituals, but they can also light claws, fangs, arms or whatever. These flames are approximately as intense as those of a torch. This Gift is taught by a fire elemental or Sun-spirit.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point. Artificial fires do half damage to the Ahroun; natural (even mystical, if from a “pure” source such as an elemental Gift) do nothing at all. The effects last for one scene.
As the Level One Metis Gift.

The wild is a harsh mistress. By calling the Mother into her heart, a Fury may tame the worst effects of weather and season. No matter how the sun or bitter the wind, the Fury feels as comfortable as springtime. This Gift is taught by a Weather-spirit.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point; the protection lasts for one day. With this Gift inactive, the werewolf remains immune to extremes of temperature, so long as those extremes aren’t severe enough to be considered attacks. The Gift also raises the damage difficulty of environmental attacks (ice storms, bonfires, cold winds) from 6 to 7. This Gift protects against temperatures only; it offers no defense against explosion, earthquake or tornado damage.

By pointing a finger or claw at a target (usually male), a Fury can force him to his knees. He may cuss up a storm, but unless he’s a strong-willed bastard, there’s nothing he can do but kneel. This Gift is taught by one of Pegasus’ brood.

System: The Fury rolls Manipulation + Intimidation against the subject’s current Willpower rating. Her target falls to his knees unless he spends a Gnosis point to resist the Gift’s effects. Other supernatural creatures can spend spirit energy (Blood, Quintessence or Glamour) to rise to their feet, but mortals remain helpless. The Gift lasts for one turn per success, and doesn’t work on spirit beings.

The werewolf, if she knows anything about her prey (mortal or spirit), can unfailingly track it down as rapidly as she can travel. She can track anywhere, salt desert, prairie or Storm Umbra alike, although an Umbral storm can erase the trail. This Gift is taught by a Wolf- or Dog-spirit.

System: No roll is required unless the target is actively hiding, in which case the player rolls Perception + Enigmas, difficulty of the target’s Wits + Stealth. If the target is a spirit, the difficulty is the spirit’s Gnosis.

By calling her Rage to the surface, a Black Fury may turn her claws barbed and venomous. Rakes from these vicious talons burn for hours. This Gift is taught by a Pain-spirit.

System: The Garou spends one Rage point with each attack. Her claws inflict their usual damage, but any Health Level penalty caused by their attacks is doubled for the scene’s duration (i.e., a Wound enemy would suffer a -4 penalty instead of the usual -2). This Gift works normally even if the target is frenzied.

The Furies are one with the Wyld in many ways. By channeling that kinship into a blast of pure chaos, a werewolf may shred a spirit’s essence, scattering its power to the Umbral winds. Sadly, this power does nothing against Wyld-spirits; it can be pretty effective against Wyrm and Weaver ones, though. This Gift is taught by a Wyld-spirit.

System: The Fury rolls her current Gnosis against difficulty 6; each success reduces a spirit’s Power by three (i.e., a spirit hit by a three-success attack loses nine Power points). Each attack costs the Garou one Gnosis point. The Armor Charm cannot prevent this damage. Unless the spirit is materialized, Spirit Ripper works only in the Umbra. It will not harm material beings in any way, although possessed ones (mockers and the like) take one aggravated Health Level per success.

Furies are healers as well as warriors. By bringing forth the Earth’s healing powers, a Fury can dispel the worst effects of Wyrm-taint and injury like cool breeze blows across the mortal and spirit worlds alike when this Gift is used.

System: The Fury rolls her permanent Gnosis and spends one point per success. The roll’s difficulty depends on how corrupted the area is.

Corruption	Difficulty
Mildly Tainted (a battlefield)	6
Tainted (fomori bunks)	7
Corrupted (a Wyrm-cult’s lodge)	8
Blighted (an Umbral Blight)	9
Hopeless (an Anchorhead to Malfeas)	10

The presence of Wyrm-creatures in the area raises the difficulty by one (maximum 10).

Each success reduces the corruption by one level. The Healing covers an area roughly 100 feet by 100 feet; a Fury can spend double the Gnosis to heal twice that area. While the Gift’s effects aren’t immediately noticeable, the place begins to “recover” from the Wyrm’s effects. In time, the Penumbra reshapes itself into a more “neutral” state. Any Wyrm-creature caught within a 20-foot radius of the Garou when she casts the Gift suffers one aggravated Health Level (nonsoakable) per success.

The Fury can actually fire her claws from her fingertips, cutting down her foes from a distance. The werewolf is incapable of using this Gift or making a claw attack next turn, for her claws must actually regenerate. This Gift is taught by a Wasp-spirit.

System: The player rolls Dexterity + Brawl. Damage is calculated as for an ordinary claw attack, while difficulty is determined by range as though the Garou were using a firearm. Medium range (no modifier) is 20 yards; double this range (40 yards) is the farthest the claws can be hurled, and the difficulty increases by one. On the other hand, attacks made at targets within a yard of the Fury are considered “point-blank” (difficulty 4).

Like the witches of legend, some Black Furies can assume the shapes of animals. This Gift allows a warrior to become any normal beast from a dove to a buffalo. The animal’s powers (flight, speed, gills, etc.) come with the form. Animal totems pass this Gift to worthy Furies, and might revoke the favor if a wolf forgets her kinship to the Wyld.

System: The Fury spends a Gnosis point and rolls Dexterity + Animal Ken. The difficulty depends on the animal she chooses; human-sized ones are easy (6), while radical changes in size or mass are difficult (7+).

By harnessing the raging currents of the Storm Umbra, a Fury with this Gift unleashes a tempest in the mortal world. It’s a dangerous trick to use; first, she must step sideways, exposing herself to the Umbral winds. Next, she sings the maelstrom into a shape, then brings that shape through the Gauntlet. The resulting storm lasts a few moments at best, but tears anything in its path to bits.

System: The Garou must be “sideways” to use this Gift. First, she rolls her Manipulation + Enigmas, difficulty of the area’s Gauntlet. Next, she spends one Rage and one Willpower. Success summons a storm of the Storyteller’s choosing (not the player’s). Obviously, the tempest’s length and severity depends on the number of successes scored; the nature of the storm can be as wildly inappropriate as the energies that create it (ice storms in the desert, lightning on a sunny day, that sort of thing).

The use of musk has long been a defense in the wild. With this Gift, the Garou can greatly amplify her own natural body odor, creating a debilitating stench. This Gift is taught by a Stinkbug- or Skunk-spirit.

System: The Garou spends one Gnosis point. For the duration of the scene, all beings within 20 feet of the Garou (provided they possess olfactory organs) subtract one from all Dice Pools as they choke and retch. However, the Garou subtracts two from all his Social Dice Pools and may be ostracized for the duration.

A Bone Gnawer with nothing more than this Gift, a few drops of water and a small pot can create food from even twigs and rocks. By placing whatever smallish objects he can find in the pot and adding enough water to moisten them, the Gnawer creates an edible (if rather rancid to the tongue) gruel. This Gift is taught by a Raccoon- or Rat-spirit.

System: The Garou rolls Wits + Survival. The difficulty depends on the objects used — 6 for twigs and deadwood, 7 for small, harmless pebbles, and up to 10 for cyanide and the like.

When a Garou is backed into a corner with nowhere to run to, she can either give up or fight. This Gift allows the Garou to focus her Rage and lash out at her opponents.

System: The player spends one Rage point and rolls Strength + Intimidation (difficulty 8). Each success grants her an additional die to her Brawl Dice Pools. When using this Gift, the werewolf automatically enters into a Berserk frenzy. This Gift lasts until combat is over or the frenzy ends.
As the Level Two Ragabash Gift.

The Gnawer can whistle up wood rot of the worst kind, decaying even the stoutest lumber in a matter of seconds. This Gift is taught by a Termite-spirit.

System: The player rolls Intelligence + Crafts (difficulty 7) and spends a Gnosis point. The number of successes determines how much wood is affected. One success rots a hitching post or plank, three successes collapses part of a floor, and five successes demolishes a trestle bridge.
As the Level Three Homid Gift.

When using this Gift, the Gnawer can change her claws to glass. Upon clawing an opponent, the glass talons tear through flesh and bone, breaking off in the target’s wounds. The Garou is incapable of using this Gift or making a claw attack next turn, for her claws must actually regenerate. This Gift is taught by a Pain-spirit.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point to make the change. If the Garou using this Gift damages her target with a claw attack, her claws break off, leaving large shards in the open wounds. The glass does an additional aggravated Health Level of damage every round the shards remain in the wound. The glass can be removed, but this takes a full turn, and the victim can’t be running around or fighting.

When scouting out the area, a Bone Gnawer quickly picks up on the nooks and crannies where good hiding places are available...just in case. By using this Gift, the Gnawer can duck into a shaded area (behind a barn, into an empty barrel) and pop out in another shady area some distance away. This Gift is taught by a Prairie dog-spirit.

System: The Garou spends one Gnosis point and rolls Manipulation + Stealth (difficulty 6). For every success scored, the Garou may increase the distance he reappears by 20 yards — one success and he reappears 20 yards away; 2 successes, 40 yards; etc.). He cannot reappear in plain sight — he must emerge from another likely hiding place.

The frontier and wilderness are sometimes more than a match for even the heartiest of Garou — but then again, the Garou are a versatile bunch. This Gift grants temporary immunity to many environmental effects, such as pressure or temperature variations. The Garou is also immune to disease and poison, as well as any corrupting effects from the touch of Wyrm entities. This Gift is taught by a Bear-spirit.

System: The Garou spends one Gnosis point and rolls Stamina + Survival (difficulty 7); the effects last one day per success. She does not need to eat, drink or rest, and she gains three extra points of Stamina, even if this takes the Attribute over 10. She also ignores wound penalties. In addition, whenever she is about to die or be defeated, she automatically regains one Willpower point.

The Bone Gnawer can open his jaws far wider than is normally physically possible, and can swallow things or beings whole. A werewolf with this Gift is even capable of gulping down an entire buffalo, or a whole pack of Black Spiral Dancers, only to regurgitate the creatures later.

System: The player rolls Stamina + Primal-Urge (difficulty 8). Beings can resist with Dexterity + Dodge. In the case of a very large being or object, one extra success is required for every Health Level the target possesses beyond the normal seven, or for every extra three feet in size over the Bone Gnawer (obviously, Crinos form is best for this Gift). Swallowed objects/beings wind up in the Umbra, in a subrealm that is a mystical “stomach” of the glutton. The Garou can belch these objects out later. The werewolf can store objects in this mystical stomach for a number of hours equal to his Stamina; after this time, the Garou automatically regurgitates them back into the physical world. The objects appear next to the Garou, and are unharmed.
As the Level One Theurge Gift.
As the Level One Philodox Gift.

The werewolf can pull the bite right out of a body. This Gift is taught by a Unicorn-spirit.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point and rolls Charisma + Empathy (difficulty of the target’s Willpower). Each success removes one of the target’s Rage points, though lost Rage can be regained normally.

The werewolf invokes the moon’s sacred powers of protection to repel his enemy’s attacks. This Gift is taught by a Lune.

System: The werewolf concentrates for one turn; the player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Stamina + Survival (difficulty 6). Each success allows the Garou to add one to his Stamina for the purpose of soaking damage (including damage from silver). The effect lasts for one scene.

The werewolf can flood a target’s senses with visions of Gaia’s true splendor. This Gift is taught by a Unicorn-spirit.

System: The player rolls Charisma + Empathy (difficulty of the target’s Willpower). As long as the target is not attacked, he stands mutely in awe for the remainder of the scene. This Gift may only be attempted once per target per scene.

The werewolf has learned to project a feeling of tranquility and friendship that spirits naturally perceive. Save for the mad or corrupt, spirits treat the Garou with courtesy. This Gift is taught by Unicorn-spirits.

System: The player rolls Charisma + Expression (difficulty 7); each success adds one die to all the Garou’s Dice Pools for interacting with any spirits in the vicinity (except those of the Wyrm). The effect lasts for one scene.
As the Level Four Lupus Gift.

The Child of Gaia can sing out the rage in people’s hearts, even bringing shapechangers, vampires and the like out of frenzy. This Gift is taught by Unicorn-spirits.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Charisma + Expression (difficulty of the target’s Willpower). The target is incapable of using Rage at all for one turn per success. What’s more, the target cannot frenzy while this Gift is in effect.

The werewolf is able to end the suffering of mad spirits in the Storm Umbra. The Gift may restore the spirit to sanity, or destroy it. This Gift only works on spirits that have been driven mad; it has no effect on Wyrm-spirits.

System: The Garou spends one Gnosis point and one Willpower point and rolls Charisma + Enigmas (difficulty 8). Each success strips 10 Power from the spirit; when the spirit reaches zero Power, the Child of Gaia can opt to cure it (restoring it to full Power and cleansing it of its madness) or, if such a cure is impossible (Storyteller’s option), to destroy it.

The Child of Gaia speaks an old word sacred to the Sun and is immediately surrounded by a sphere of blazing sunlight. Those Wyrm-creatures who fear the sun may flee before the bright shining Garou. This Gift is taught by a spirit allied to Helios, the Sun Celestine.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point; the effect lasts for one scene. The damage of any of the werewolf’s hand-to-hand attacks is increased by two dice; alternately, the Garou can cause aggravated damage when in Homid or Glabro form. Anyone directly facing the werewolf adds three to all attack difficulties because of the glare, and vampires within 20 yards suffer damage as if exposed to true sunlight.
As the Level One Homid Gift.

The Fianna have a long practice of brewing whatever they can find to come up with new forms of libations. Sometimes this has meant a fresh batch of hooch that was outright dangerous — but the Fianna have learned to adapt. A Garou with this Gift takes no damage from poisons or diseases of any sort, including many Wyrm-toxins. Some Toad-spirits and Plant-spirits may teach this Gift.

System: The Garou rolls Stamina + Survival (difficulty 6) to resist the disease or poison; only one success is required.

Some people only hear what they want to hear, and this Gift lets a Fianna help them along by making anything she says what the target wants to hear. Whatever the werewolf says, even gibberish, will be heartily agreed with, although only the target of the Gift will know what she thinks the Garou is saying. (The Garou won’t have a clue what the target thinks she’s saying.) Any one of the many Trickster-spirits may teach this Gift.

System: The Garou spends one Gnosis point and rolls Wits + Expression (difficulty of the victim’s Wits + Alertness). The effect lasts one turn per success.

The Fianna say that the wail of the Banshee, a terrifying spirit of the dead, means that someone will die before the sun rises. A Garou who learns this Gift may emit an unnatural howl which seems to echo across the Gauntlet, filling all who hear it with dread whether they recognize its significance or not. To learn this Gift, a Fianna must seek out a Banshee where the Middle Lands touch the Underworld.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point; the Fianna throws back his head and howls. All who hear the howl, friend or foe, must make Willpower rolls (difficulty 8; 6 for friends of the Garou) or flee in terror for one turn per success.

In the Old World, the Fianna learned how to find the secret places where faeries hid riches in the earth. This Gift allows a Garou to sense deposits of gold and silver within a general area (a couple of mile radius). This Gift is taught by Rock-spirits.

System: The player spends a point of Willpower and rolls Perception + Alertness (difficulty 7). Each success gives the Garou more information. For example, a single success would let the Fianna know that valuable minerals are in the area; two successes would indicate the type, while three would give the Fianna a general indication of where the precious metal was located. With five successes, the Garou could walk over to a stream bed and pluck out a gold-covered rock. This Gift cannot locate gold or silver in an area where the metals aren’t present. Fianna can only attempt this Gift once in any given place.
As the Level Three Homid Gift.

When a Fianna invokes this Gift, one of her eyes emits a baleful crimson glow, seeming to burn with the fires of damnation. Anyone without the resolve to meet Balor’s Gaze without flinching suffers wracking pains. A Pain-spirit teaches this Gift.

System: The player spends one Gnosis and one Rage point, and rolls Perception + Occult (difficulty 8). For the remainder of the scene, any enemy at whom the Fianna glances must equal or exceed the Garou’s successes on a Willpower roll (difficulty 8) or double over in agony. Such a foe is treated as “Crippled” (-5 to actions) for the scene, even though the foe takes no actual wounds. Already Crippled enemies are effectively Incapacitated. If a foe successfully resists, she is immune to the Gift for the duration of the scene.

This Gift conjures a static illusion which can fully affect all senses. Mirage-spirits, Water-spirits and Trickster-spirits may teach this Gift.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point for each 10-foot area to be covered by the illusion and rolls Intelligence + Expression (difficulty 6). All in the vicinity must match or exceed the number of successes with a Perception + Alertness roll (difficulty 6) or believe that the illusion is real.

The Garou may grow up to three times her normal size or shrink to the size of a small puppy. Chimerlings teach this Gift.

System: The Garou spends one Gnosis point and rolls Stamina + Primal-Urge (difficulty 8). The effects last one hour per success. When larger, the Garou gains +3 Strength (maximum of 10) and one extra Health Level for every 100% increase in size: When smaller, the Garou retains her normal Strength and abilities, but her Lupus form is as innocuous as a puppy.

The Fianna can channel all his power into a desperate frenzy, the likes of which normal humans have never seen — and lived. The werewolf draws some of his own blood and tastes it to enter this state. Once he’s been driven into this frenzy, not even armies stand much of a chance of stopping him.

System: The player spends three Rage points and rolls Stamina + Primal-Urge, difficulty 4. The Fianna goes into a warp-frenzy for one turn for each success. While in this frenzied state, the Fianna takes no penalties from wounds, gains +4 Strength, +3 Dexterity, +4 Stamina and three extra Bruised Health Levels — all above and beyond any normal Attribute modifiers for forms. In addition, he regains at least one Rage point per turn. At the end of this frenzy, the Fianna takes five Health Levels of aggravated damage — that can’t be soaked. If he survives, his Rage and Willpower drop to 1 each until he’s rested for four hours.
As the Level One Ahroun Gift.

The Garou employing this Gift knows if anything suffering a Wyrm-taint come onto his property. This Gift is taught by an Owl-spirit.

System: The player spends one Gnosis to establish this early warning system, and one Gnosis per day to maintain it. To detect a Wyrmling entering the area, the character rolls Wits + Perception (difficulty 7). Each success allows the character to more clearly sense where the threat is coming from; one success might tell him that a threat is coming from the east, whereas five successes could pinpoint the exact location of the threat.

The werewolf lets out a fearsome roar, terrifying opponents and cowing them into submission. Garou hearing the roar are often cowed into submission; humans are flooded with racial memories of the Impergium and are often overcome with instinctive terror. This Gift is taught by a Wolf-spirit.

System: This Gift works automatically against humans and animals. Any human seeing the Garou after hearing this cry reacts as if their Willpower were only 2 for purposes of the Delirium Chart. Against supernatural opponents, the Garou rolls Charisma + Intimidation (difficulty of the target’s Wits + 3). Each success removes one from the number of dice the opponent can use in the next turn, as the victim is overwhelmed with fear. This Gift takes a full turn to invoke.

The Garou may slow his fleeing foes, preventing them from escaping his wrath. This Gift is taught by one of Fenris’ brood.

System: The Garou spends a turn concentrating; the player rolls Charisma + Intimidation against a difficulty of the target’s Willpower. If successful, the Garou may halve the speed of that individual for one scene.

The Get draws on incredible strength, wreaking terrible damage on his opponents. This Gift is taught by a Wolf-spirit.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point and one Rage point; he then rolls Willpower, difficulty 8. The Garou’s Strength doubles for one turn per success. Afterward, the character is exhausted (Physical Attributes 1, and Willpower halved) until he can rest for at least one hour.

The werewolf calls on Fenris, summoning Strength and Rage from the Wolf-God and striking at his enemies with increased fury. This Gift is taught by an avatar of Fenris.

System: This Gift may only be used when a Get is outnumbered and facing overwhelming odds. The player spends two Gnosis and one Willpower. He then rolls Rage, difficulty 5. The Get’s Strength increases by three and his Rage by five; the effects of this Gift last for one scene. The character can frenzy, but is incapable of falling into the Thrall of the Wyrm, as he is already under the influence of Fenris. The Rage of Fenris automatically fails in any attempts where the character is not facing at least two-to-one odds.

The Garou plants herself on a patch of ground and channels the very force of Gaia through her body, essentially becoming one with the earth. She may not retreat or even move from her chosen spot, and no force on Earth can move her, but she gains tremendous benefits in exchange. Only when all her foes are defeated may the Garou leave her chosen area. This Gift is taught by an earth elemental.

System: The player rolls Willpower, difficulty 8. Each success increases all the Get’s Dice Pools by one; she may not be surprised and all attacks against her are considered frontal attacks.

The werewolf howls at an opponent with impossible force, shattering bones and bruising flesh. This Gift is taught by a Wolf-spirit.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point and one Willpower point. He must also make a Rage roll, difficulty 6. Each success causes two levels of aggravated damage to one opponent. This Gift may only be used against minions of the Wyrm, and any attempts to use the Gift improperly cause the character to suffer the damage he would have inflicted, in addition to costing the character one permanent Glory renown.

The Get’s teeth become incredibly sharp and her jaws impossibly strong, allowing her to mangle limbs or even sever them completely. This Gift is taught by an avatar of Fenris.

System: The player spends one Rage point and rolls Strength + Medicine against a difficulty of the opponent’s Stamina + 3. The Garou’s next bite, if it hits, will crush and mangle one of the opponent’s limbs, inflicting one additional level of damage above what the character rolls. The target’s limb is effectively useless for the remainder of the scene, or longer if the target can’t regenerate. If five or more successes are scored on the Strength + Medicine roll, the limb is completely severed.

The Garou chooses to win against an opponent no matter the odds, and calls on Fenris for aid. The Garou continues fighting long after he should be dead, and doesn’t fall until his enemy (or enemies) dies with him. Obviously, this Gift is only ever used once. This Gift is taught by an avatar of Fenris.

System: The Garou concentrates for one full turn, and then expends all of his Gnosis in summoning Fenris’ favor. Once that’s done, he suffers no penalties for his wounds, and won’t fall until his foe is dead. Despite the power of this Gift, the Get can’t seek an enemy he is not already in direct combat with at the time. Once the battle is over, an avatar of Fenris comes down to devour the body of the dead hero, taking all of his worldly possessions with him. This Gift is only used when the character is already at the brink of death, with no alternative for winning the battle. Improper use of this Gift (i.e., attempting to settle a personal vendetta instead of battling a minion of the Wyrm) immediately kills the Garou who attempts to summon Fenris.

The Iron Rider can perceive and speak to the spirits in simplistic machines, ordering them around to the Iron Rider’s tastes. He can compel doors to unbolt, hinges to swing open or shut, levers to flip and so on. This Gift is taught by any technological spirit.

System: The player spends a Willpower point and rolls Manipulation + Crafts (difficulty 7). The werewolf’s control lasts for the duration of the scene.

This Gift allows the werewolf to sense the presence and influence of the Weaver. The sense is supernatural, but tends to manifest as a thick oily smell or a sticky feeling on the skin, as if the Garou had just walked through cobwebs.

System: The player rolls Perception + Crafts. The difficulty depends on the concentration and the range of the encroachment. Sensing a hidden mine shaft 10 feet away (difficulty 6) is easier than detecting a rifle 100 yards away (difficulty 9). Multiple successes can indicate details of the Weaver-influence.

The werewolf can temporarily shift his fur into steel, gaining an added measure of protection. This Gift is taught by Iron-spirits or earth elementals.

System: The player spends a Willpower point and rolls Stamina + Science (difficulty 7). The Garou’s soak pool is increased by one die, plus another die for each success; the change lasts for a scene or until the Garou decides to shift back to normal. This Gift is only marginally effective in Homid form, and hairless metis can’t learn this Gift. While this Gift is active, the Iron Rider suffers a +1 difficulty to all Dexterity rolls.

The Garou with this Gift has learned the ways of the Weaver-spirits and can commune with them. By placing her ear against a railroad track, she can hear anything happening anywhere along that rail as if she were actually there. An Iron-spirit teaches this Gift.

System: By rolling Perception + Science, the werewolf can attempt to listen to the Iron-spirits in the rail. The difficulty depends on the distance away from the target area. The difficulty starts at 4 with a range of 20 miles, and increases by one for every 10 miles beyond 20 (40 miles would be difficulty 6, etc.). The number of successes also reflects the amount of details the Garou can discern from the Iron-spirits’ constant chatter.

The Iron Riders were the first tribe to learn about the force of magnetism, and have tapped its power to suit their own ends. With a mere gesture, the werewolf can repel all ferrous metals in his immediate area, sending them flying.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Strength + Science, difficulty 7. All objects made of ferrous metal within 30 feet of the Iron Rider immediately fly 20 feet farther away from the Garou. This is no good against lead bullets or klaives, but works very well against pistols and knives. The Garou can repel objects weighing up to five pounds for every success on the roll; three successes repel any iron or steel weighing 15 pounds or less, for instance. If an object is in somebody’s grasp (drawn pistols, for instance), the object’s owner can try to hold onto it by making a resisted Strength roll against the Iron Rider.
As the Level Three Homid Gift.

The Iron Rider may take the exact likeness of a particular human or wolf, or even another werewolf. This is taught by a spirit of Illusion.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point and rolls Charisma + Performance (difficulty 8); the effect lasts for one day per success.

When between the rails of a railroad line, the Iron Rider can draw on the strength of the locomotive itself.

System: This Gift need not be activated; its effects are automatic once learned. The Garou can run at double speed without tiring, and gains an extra die to all her Physical Attributes while touching railroad tracks. When on open ground, her abilities return to normal.

The Iron Rider can actually walk among humans without accidentally evoking the Curse or the Delirium. This Gift allows him to maintain a “normal” life or even a family; however, the effects don’t last all that long. This Gift is taught by a homid Ancestor-spirit.

System: The player rolls Manipulation + Subterfuge (difficulty 7). The number of successes indicates the number of hours that the effect lasts.

As the favored tribe of the Weaver, Iron Riders are actually capable of imposing order on even the Storm Umbra. This Gift allows an Iron Rider elder to calm and even dispel Umbral storms by countering their Wyld energies with the power of the Weaver. Naturally, several tribes, the Black Furies and Red Talons in particular, resent werewolves who use this Gift to advance the Weaver’s influence.

System: The player spends two Gnosis and rolls Gnosis, difficulty 8. The number of successes determines how effective this Gift is; one success can slow a strong Umbral wind down or banish an Umbral breeze, while five successes can convert a full-blown Umbral storm into dead calm in a matter of seconds. If used against Wyld-spirits, the roll is against a difficulty of the spirit’s Gnosis, and the spirit loses four Power for every success. The werewolf cannot use this Gift more than once against the same storm or spirit.
As the Level One Galliard Gift.

Of all Garou, Red Talons are closest to their wolf brethren. This Gift allows a Red Talon to hunt in the same way as a wild wolf. The Garou can examine a group and determine which are the strongest and weakest members. This Gift is taught by a Wolf-spirit, or a Gaffling of the Hunt.

System: The player rolls Perception + Primal Urge (difficulty 7). If successful, the Red Talon learns which individual is strongest and which is weakest in the group she watches. If the Garou subsequently attacks this group, she gains one extra attack die against the weakest individual only.

The Garou can reduce the mental faculties of a target to those of an animal for a short time. The victim doesn’t necessarily become less intelligent, but human thinking (logic, mathematics, language and the like) becomes impossible. This Gift is taught by an avatar of Griffin.

System: The Garou rolls Manipulation + Empathy against a difficulty of the target’s Willpower. The target is affected for one turn per success.

This Gift enables the Red Talon to evoke deep-rooted fear in her enemies. The werewolf’s howl sounds as if it comes from several wolves (or Garou), giving even Wyrm-creatures reason to pause.

System: The Garou rolls Stamina + Expression (difficulty 7). If the roll is successful, anyone who wishes to approach the Garou must make a Willpower roll (difficulty 6) to overcome instinctive fear. Each success after the first makes it sound as if there is an additional howler and adds one to the difficulty of the Willpower roll (maximum difficulty of 10).

Developed by desert-dwelling Red Talons beyond the frontier, this new Gift is otherwise not widely known. The werewolf can call up a small twister of wind and sand to blind or scare opponents. The wind is not large enough to knock full-sized targets over, but will cause them some distress. This Gift is taught by a spirit of the great desert.

System: The Garou spends a Gnosis point and rolls Charisma + Primal-Urge, difficulty 6. Each success means the squall “attacks” the Talon’s opponents for one turn. Opponents besieged by the squall add two to the difficulty of all dice rolls, and communication and vision become almost impossible over any distance.

This Gift allows a Garou who is at home in a certain area of wilderness (has spent some time hunting and sleeping there) to disorient others who enter the region. Humans and other travelers are completely confused by this Gift, losing their bearings and becoming lost. Compasses and maps only serve to confuse the issue further, and even werewolves aren’t immune. This Gift is taught by any wilderness spirit.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Intelligence + Primal-Urge (difficulty 7 against lupus). He may affect up to a two-mile radius for every success. Other werewolves can avoid the effects by rolling Intelligence + Primal-Urge themselves and obtaining equal or more successes than the Red Talon. Humans and creatures of the Wyrm get no such chance. The effect lasts for four hours.

The Red Talon turns the surrounding area into an unstable and viscous substance which entraps and hinders her foes. The hapless victims can suffocate if not rescued. The Gift is taught by earth elementals.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Manipulation + Primal-Urge against a difficulty of 7. Each success converts a 10-foot area of ground into dangerous muck. Anyone in the area but the Garou has their movement halved; weak individuals begin to sink. Trapped targets can’t attempt any activities requiring speed or balance, and they may eventually suffocate.

A torrent of stinging and blinding sand encompasses the enemies of the Garou. This Gift is best performed in the desert, but can be attempted in any area with loose dirt, twigs, sleet or the like. This Gift is taught by a Desert-spirit or elemental.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Manipulation + Survival (difficulty 7 in the desert; 9 anywhere else). The number of successes determines the number of minutes in which the targets can do nothing but cover themselves and seek shelter as best they can from the stinging sand. A botch indicates that the Talon himself is caught in the blast.

The Garou calls upon her intimate connection to Gaia, inspiring the land itself to enact righteous vengeance on her foes. Trees, rocks and other natural features hinder, hamper and batter the Garou’s enemies while the Red Talon watches. It’s said that an avatar of Gaia Herself is the only spirit who teaches this Gift.

System: The player spends one Gnosis and one Rage, and rolls Charisma + Enigmas against a difficulty of the area’s Gauntlet. The Red Talon pleads with Gaia to cleanse the land, and Gaia responds with whatever weapons the area has to offer. The game effects are left to the Storyteller.

The Talon can completely transform another being into a wolf. If the target is a Garou, he becomes trapped in his Lupus form for the duration of the current scene. If the target is a human or animal, it becomes a normal wolf forever. The Gift is taught by a Wolf-spirit.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Gnosis against a difficulty of the target’s Willpower; she only needs one success.

The werewolf seems surrounded by a dark halo of strength and command. Others find it impossible to read her aura or detect any of her weaknesses; she reveals no flaws whatsoever. This Gift is taught by an Ancestor-spirit.

System: The player rolls Charisma + Subterfuge (difficulty 7). This must be rolled for each new encounter or situation in which the Garou finds herself.

The Shadow Lord can pick apart his enemy’s weaknesses, gaining a distinct edge in a fight. This Gift is taught by a Stormcrow.

System: The Garou concentrates for one turn; the player rolls Perception + Empathy (difficulty of the target’s Wits + Subterfuge). Success allows the Garou to do an extra die of damage against the target. Extra successes allow him to discern additional vulnerabilities; five successes grant the Garou knowledge of all of the target’s flaws. This knowledge lasts for a scene.

The Shadow Lord may clap her hands together, generating a shockwave that can stun unwary foes. This Gift is taught by a Stormcrow.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point. All those within 10 feet of the Shadow Lord, friend and foe alike, must score at least one success on a Willpower roll (difficulty 8) or be stunned and unable to act for one turn; on a botch, they are stunned for the duration of the scene. The Garou must be in Homid, Glabro or Crinos form to employ this power.
As the Level Two Children of Gaia Gift.

The Shadow Lord can appear to grow larger, becoming a shadowy, terrible version of himself. This frightening change in aspect cows and intimidates any onlookers. This Gift is taught by a Stormcrow.

System: The Garou concentrates for a turn; the player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Manipulation + Intimidation (difficulty 7). If successful, anyone who means the Shadow Lord harm and actually sees this terrifying vision must score at least as many successes as the Garou on a Willpower roll (difficulty 8). Failure means that the victims must spend Willpower to attack, take action against or even verbally oppose the Garou. The Shadow Lord holds no power over his victims; they’re merely too spooked to offer him any resistance. This effect lasts for the rest of the scene.

The Shadow Lord can wound a foe by striking his very shadow. This form of attack is much more difficult to dodge, and can be a most convenient attack form in some conditions — such as sundown. This Gift is taught by a Night-spirit.

System: The werewolf must first spit into her opponent’s shadow and spend a Gnosis point. For the remainder of the scene, the Shadow Lord may strike at her foe’s shadow to wound him. She may only use fetish weapons, silver or natural weaponry such as claws to attack in this fashion; more mundane attacks don’t carry any impact. The victim has two fewer dice to dodge attacks targeted at his shadow, and can’t parry them at all. The Storyteller may even give the shadow’s attackers extra dice in situations where the shadow prevents an even more convenient target; for example, if the victim is standing in an alleyway out of reach, but his shadow falls at the Garou’s feet.

The Shadow Lord may curse the next wound he inflicts on a target, making it bleed profusely. This Gift is taught by a Pain-spirit.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Strength + Medicine (difficulty of the target’s Stamina + 3). If the roll succeeds, the target bleeds, losing one Health Level per turn for a duration of one turn per success. This is not aggravated damage.

The Garou, by force of command, draws from another’s anger to feed his own. This Gift is taught by a Stormcrow.

System: The player rolls Wits + Intimidation (difficulty of the target’s Willpower). For a number of turns equal to the successes scored, the victim loses one Rage point per turn, while the Shadow Lord gains that Rage point. This may be attempted only once per target per scene.

The werewolf may summon a shadowy duplicate of himself, or even multiple shadows, to stand beside him in battle. These shadow-duplicates possess the same powers as he does, and can fight just as fiercely. This Gift is taught by a spirit of Night or a Chimerling.

System: The player must spend a variable number of Gnosis points and succeed in a Gnosis roll (difficulty 8). For each Gnosis point spent, the Shadow Lord summons an exact duplicate of himself to fight by his side. These Garou have identical Attributes and Abilities, but not fetishes; they may not use Gnosis or Willpower, and each has only one Health Level (any attack that is not soaked destroys it). The shadow-Garou last for one scene.

Although Shadow Lords have no qualms about bending the truth to serve their own ends, they dislike being lied to, especially by lessers. This Gift allows the werewolf to force truth from a subject; if the target lies to the Garou, wounds appear across his body. He who prevaricates, dies. This Gift is taught by a Pain-spirit.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Charisma + Intimidation, difficulty of the subject’s Willpower. If he succeeds, the link is established. From then on, every fib the subject tells inflicts a Health Level of aggravated damage on the liar; this damage is not soakable. Even half-truths open small, stinging but not life-threatening wounds. Garou may overcome the effect by spending as much Gnosis as the interrogator rolled successes. Unless the victim succeeds at a Willpower roll, difficulty 8, he will probably start blurting out the truth after the first wound or two — particularly if the Shadow Lord tells him that keeping silent will kill him, too (a lie, but in most Shadow Lords’ eyes, a justifiable one). This Gifts lasts for a scene.
As the Level One Metis Gift.

The Garou may double her landspeed. This Gift is taught by a Horse- or Roadrunner-spirit.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point; and the effect lasts one scene.
As the Level Two Ragabash Gift.

The Garou may run for an extended period without needing rest, food or water. After this Gift expires, the Strider must sleep for an entire week. This Gift is taught by Camel-spirits in other lands; it’s taught by Wolf-spirits in the Savage West.

System: The player spends a point of Gnosis and the werewolf may do nothing except run for the duration of the Gift. By spending an additional Gnosis point, the player may bestow Messenger’s Fortitude on another being. The Gift lasts as many days as the (breed form) Stamina of the character affected.

The Garou adapts to accommodate any surroundings. While under the effects of this Gift, she is unaffected by pressure, temperature, atmospheric conditions and other hostile environmental factors. Poison and disease likewise have no effect on her, though she may be harmed by concentrated flame or radiation. This Gift is taught by Bear- and Armadillo-spirits.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Stamina + Survival (difficulty 7). The effects last for one hour per success and may be extended by spending another Gnosis and rolling again. The Garou may also bestow this Gift upon another; each affected companion requires a Gnosis point expenditure, as well.

The Strider can jump incredible distances in one bound. This Gift is taught by Jackrabbit-spirits.

System: The player spends a Willpower point and rolls Strength + Athletics (difficulty 6). Each success enables the werewolf to leap 100 feet.

By standing in a particular area of the wilderness, the character can commune with the spirits of that area, thus getting an overview of what exists or has happened in the area — rough population, secret trails, places of note and so on. This Gift is taught by an Owl- or Fox-spirit.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Perception + Survival (difficulty 6). The amount of information garnered depends on the number of successes. On a botch, the spirits lie.

The werewolf may run at 10 times normal speed. The Garou must do nothing but run for the duration of this Gift: eight full hours. After the effects of this Gift wear off, the Strider is famished and must eat immediately or risk frenzy (and worse…). This Gift is taught by an Air-spirit or Lune.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Stamina + Athletics (difficulty 7). Failure means the Gift simply does not work, while a botch only bestows the hunger of the aftermath on the poor Garou…

This power only works at night when the moon is at least partially visible. The werewolf opens a special, temporary Moon Bridge, which allows instantaneous travel to her destination. This Gift is only taught by Lunes.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point for every 100 miles he wishes the Garou to travel, and rolls Intelligence + Alertness (in which the difficulty increases with the distance of the journey and the sketchiness with which the destination is known — a far journey to an unknown place would demand a difficulty of 10). Success enables the werewolf to travel to that place instantly. Upon arrival, the Garou will be disoriented for a turn unless the player scores at least three successes. A botch means that the character is hurled through the Umbra in a random direction, arriving far from his destination (or somewhere inconvenient, like at the bottom of a river). The Garou may take others with her at a Gnosis cost of one per person.

The Garou may pierce the Gauntlet and instantly transport herself into or out of the Umbra at will; she has no need for a reflective surface. This Gift is taught by an Owl-spirit.

System: The Garou simply wills herself to step sideways. She does not have to make a Gnosis roll and is never “caught” while using this Gift.

The werewolf using this Gift gives others pause by simply entering a room. Crowded rooms will fall silent and the weak-hearted will fall back from the Garou’s very presence. This Gift is taught by a Falcon-spirit.

System: The player rolls Appearance + Intimidation (difficulty 7). The number of successes indicates the level of attention the Garou attracts. One or two successes indicates the werewolf has been noticed; three or more successes indicates the targets are seriously impressed by her appearance. If the player scores more successes than a witness’s Willpower, that person must retreat from the werewolf’s majestic presence.
As the Level One Metis Gift.

The Fang proves her right to rule and mastery over a target by assuming a regal bearing. This Gift is taught by a Falcon-spirit.

System: The player rolls Appearance + Empathy (difficulty of the target’s Willpower). If this roll is successful, the difficulties of all Social rolls made by the Garou against that target are reduced by one, and the target’s difficulty to hit the Garou in combat is increased by one. This Gift is ineffective against Garou of higher Rank.
As the Level Two Children of Gaia Gift.

The Garou reveals that he is Gaia’s Chosen Warrior and the instrument of Her vengeance to his foes. Wyrm creatures flee in fear unless they can overcome their terror. This gift is taught by an avatar of Gaia Herself.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Charisma + Intimidation (difficulty 6). All Wyrm minions (including vampires with Humanity scores lower than 7) in the vicinity must make Willpower rolls (difficulty 7) or flee in fear for the duration of the scene.
As the Level Three Ahroun Gift.

The Garou may invoke Gaia’s patronage whenever he attempts to do something which may fail without Her guidance. This Gift is taught by Falcon-spirits.

System: Before rolling for the action in question, the player may spend a point of Gnosis. If any botches turn up on the dice roll, they are ignored; do not count them toward the successes or failures of the roll.

The werewolf may command other Garou, even Black Spiral Dancers, to do his bidding. This Gift is taught by a Falcon-spirit.

System: The player rolls Charisma + Leadership (difficulty of the target’s Wits + 3). If the roll is successful, the Garou may then issue a command to the target, who must carry out the command (unless it is blatantly suicidal). This Gift affects only Garou.

The Silver Fang transforms her entire body into pure silver, becoming a virtually invincible warrior. This Gift is taught by Lunes and Ancestor-spirits.

System: The Garou concentrates for an entire turn and her player spends a Gnosis point. Using this Gift confers immunity to silver upon the user. In addition, the Gift user is assumed to have the equivalent of the Level Three Ahroun Gift, Silver Claws. She also gains two extra Stamina levels and one extra Health Level (if she suffers only one Health Level worth of damage in combat, she is unharmed after Luna’s Might expires). This Gift lasts one scene.

The Fang prevents her victim from using any special or supernatural abilities. This Gift is commonly used to teach young Garou their place in addition to besting foes. It is taught by a Falcon-spirit.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point and rolls Gnosis (difficulty of the victim’s Stamina + 3). Each success removes all special abilities (aggravated damage, claws, Gifts, vampire Disciplines and Blood Pool, mage Spheres, regenerative powers, special attacks, etc.) from the victim for one turn.

The Garou is able to walk along any narrow or precarious path without slipping or falling. This includes rail lines, mountain passes, ropes and telegraph lines, even when they’re slippery or wet. This Gift is taught by strange Wind-spirits known to the Stargazers.

System: No rolls are made nor points spent to traverse a narrow path. Difficulties of climbing are reduced by three. The effect lasts for one scene.

With this Gift a Stargazer can sense the threads woven through reality by the Weaver. The werewolf thus knows if the effects of the Weaver have been felt in an area, but also senses the direction in which those spirits head, and perhaps for what purpose. This Gift is taught by a Spider-spirit.

System: The player rolls Perception + Enigmas (difficulty 6) and the character concentrates on his surroundings. The Garou gets a glimpse into the Penumbra, but to see only the webs strung by the Weaver and its minions. The character then knows if the Weaver has affected an area recently, if it still does and sometimes if it will in the near future. On a roll of Wits + Enigmas (difficulty 8) the character gets a sense of how the Weaver affects the area, and an inkling of the repercussions of that effect.

Stargazers have used this Gift to sense where railroads, telegraph wires and other trappings of civilization will penetrate the West. The power can also be used to know where the Storm Eater has touched the Penumbra and the material world, and the Gift might be used to follow the trail of the Storm Eater or those of its creations.

The Garou balances her body weight and makes each step without leaving a track. The werewolf may also walk at normal speeds over quicksand, water, mud, snow or ice without slipping or falling. This Gift is taught by the spirits of small animals (mice, rabbits, sparrows), animals often overlooked by other Garou but honored by Stargazers.

System: The Garou concentrates for one turn and the player rolls Dexterity + Athletics (difficulty 6). If the surface can’t be crossed in a few turns, more rolls may be required during travel.

The Garou may, after meditating, cleanse her mind of all distractions and passions to focus on specific tasks. This Gift is taught by a Stargazer Ancestor-spirit.

System: The Garou concentrates for a few minutes and the player rolls Wits + Enigmas (difficulty 8). Each success converts one point of Rage into Willpower.

The Garou transcends the limits of her physical body and gains complete control of her bodily functions. Asian and African Stargazers who work as or live among mine laborers sometimes use this Gift to test mines for gas. The Gift is also used to withstand the abuses of cultural persecution in the West, not to mention survive adverse conditions. This Gift is taught by a Bear-spirit.

System: The Garou concentrates for several minutes and the player spends two Willpower points. The werewolf ignores pain and controls his bodily functions for one turn for each point of permanent Willpower. While this Gift is in effect, a character can slow his breathing so that he’s immune to the effects of gasses. He can slow his metabolism so that poisons are ineffective, or he can ignore wound penalties (though Health Levels are lost normally). The character’s body does not collapse; he performs actions normally, but much more efficiently than usual.

The Garou bestows a vision upon a person that exposes the subject to the true nature of his spirit. The nature of this vision differs with each individual, but it always confronts him with the good or corruption or evil of his ways. Whether the vision has any impression on the subject is also determined by the individual; some are completely at one with their natures, good or evil. This Gift is taught by a Crow-spirit.

System: The character must touch the target; the player rolls Perception + Enigmas (difficulty 7) and spends a point of Gnosis. The number of successes rolled indicates the degree to which the subject is exposed to his true nature. One success might make a rail baron understand that his company is bringing hard times as well as “progress.” Five successes might send a subject reeling with visions of the harm he’s caused by stealing land deeds from families. If more successes are rolled than the subject has Willpower, he undergoes a catharsis and collapses for 10 minus Willpower in turns. Whether the subject undergoes a change of personality must be roleplayed. A person who willingly embraced the Wyrm may not care about the repercussions of his actions, while a sheriff who has spiraled into corruption might stand up and mend his ways.

By touching an opponent in combat, a Stargazer can break the combatant’s will and drive him from the fray or force him to surrender. This Gift was learned from the Pure Ones, who no longer have time or patience for such gentle tactics, and is taught by a Pure One Ancestor-spirit.

System: The character touches his opponent in combat. The player makes a normal Dexterity + Brawl (or Melee) roll and spends one point of Gnosis. No damage is inflicted in the “attack,” but if the successes rolled to hit exceed the victim’s current Willpower score, the opponent loses his fighting spirit.
